rulings.Case Title: ELVIN GAZZANO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 8, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Steam and Plumbing Service Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained as speculative and lacking foundation. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Whittaker¿s depositions are sustained on substantive grounds as it does not meet former testimony exception or declaration against interest exception. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: WOODROW THOMAS VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 8, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Qualitex Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Plaintiff¿s objections to Ava Campagna¿s declaration are sustained. Plaintiff¿s objections to defendant¿s Notice of Errata, which amended Ms. Campagna¿s declaration to include an attachment, are sustained. However, even if the court could consider Ms. Campagna¿s declaration, without or with its attachment, when considered together, plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration, Ms. Campagna¿s declaration and defendant¿s responses to standard interrogatories create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing Sun Glow press pads attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration and to its own responses to standard interrogatories would be overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: KIMBALL SANCLEMENTE VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 8, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant BNSF Railway Company¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied as untimely. Defendant¿s proof of service demonstrates that it electronically served its motion on June 19, 2013. Electronic service on such date failed to provide proper notice required by C.C.P. 437c(a), C.C.P. 1010.6 and CRC 2.251. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
